mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls reduces labor market slack, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising PAYEMS months do not exceed baseline median by at least 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month acceleration in the federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary tightening that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during triggered tightening episodes are not significantly lower than non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies aggregate demand shocks and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns in walk-forward tests, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply-side bottlenecks and demand exhaustion, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn adverse.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral during triggered supply contractions, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically lifts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as analysts update consensus forecasts.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows, or forward returns in positive GDP growth quarters do not statistically differ from flat or negative GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 6.0% signals tight labor market conditions that historically precede wage-driven consumption growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative or flat when UNRATE crosses below 6.0%, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and CPI indicate a stagflationary policy stance that erodes real corporate margins and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise concurrently, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month surge in housing starts during a low-rate environment signals strong forward-looking business investment, which lifts construction-related equity sectors and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero during triggered housing surges, or the signal's hit rate falls below 45% in walk-forward validation.
